column2: What's a Nice Girl Like You Doing in a Place Like This?
column5: April 30, 2023 Criterion Channel Martin Scorsese is obviously a great filmmaker, but most recently, he's been one of the main voices of inspiration in terms of what he has literally said. "Break open the medium!" "Take your phone out and make a movie!" "Film what's on your mind, and turn it into a story!" He's full of wisdom for filmmaking confidence, so I decided instead of watching one of his features, to watch a student short film of his. It's awesome. Well made in all aspects, funny, and personal. It's what student short films should strive to be. It reminded me of French New Wave, Disney animated shorts, and the newsreel opening of Citizen Kane. Hopefully I can make Scorsese proud one day.

column2: The Wonderful Story of Henry Sugar
If Every Girl Had a Diary

column5:
January 13, 2024

Criterion Channel

After watching, I felt guilty about considering watching something to cheer myself up. Not because this movie says "you should feel ashamed for having not suffered this" or anything like that, I just feel that way after witnessing the reality, that I'm able to freely watch something entertaining when so many other people don't have this kind of distraction. I mean this as a credit to the movie's effectiveness.

Also a testament to cinema's important function of "archiving" history. Images of concentration camps and the events surrounding them captured forever on film, it's all real.
